title: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia
chief_complaint: Newborn with ambiguous genitalia (clitoromegaly, fused labia). Now presenting at 2 weeks with vomiting, weight loss, dehydration, and lethargy.

physical_exam.General: Lethargic, hypotonic, poorly responsive. Severe dehydration.
physical_exam.Genitalia: Ambiguous: Clitoromegaly (enlarged clitoris resembling small penis). Fused labioscrotal folds. Single urogenital sinus (no separate urethral and vaginal openings). Hyperpigmentation of genitalia.
physical_exam.Skin: Generalized hyperpigmentation (increased ACTH → increased MSH). Hyperpigmented areolas, gums, and genital area.

hidden_data.cmp: Na 118 (severe hyponatremia), K+ 8.0 (life-threatening hyperkalemia), Glucose 35 (hypoglycemia), HCO3 12 (metabolic acidosis)
hidden_data.cortisol: Cortisol 1.5 mcg/dL (very low - normal >18 in stress)
hidden_data.17_ohp: 17-OH Progesterone 15,000 ng/dL (massively elevated - normal <200 in newborns)
hidden_data.aldosterone: Aldosterone 5 ng/dL (inappropriately low for hyperreninemia and hyperkalemia)
hidden_data.genetic: CYP21A2 gene mutation (21-hydroxylase deficiency) - classic salt-wasting form
hidden_data.pelvic_us: Uterus and ovaries present. Normal female internal reproductive anatomy.

correct_diagnosis: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (21-Hydroxylase Deficiency) - Classic Salt-Wasting Form in 46,XX Female
key_learning_points: CAH = autosomal recessive disorder of adrenal steroidogenesis. 21-hydroxylase deficiency = 95% of cases. CYP21A2 gene mutation. Prevalence 1:15,000

Pathophysiology: 21-hydroxylase deficiency → impaired cortisol and aldosterone synthesis → shunting of precursors to androgen pathway → excess androgens. Low cortisol → ACTH elevation → adrenal hyperplasia

Classic salt-wasting (75%): Complete enzyme deficiency. Presents in first 2 weeks with adrenal crisis (hyponatremia, hyperkalemia, hypoglycemia, metabolic acidosis, shock). Ambiguous genitalia in 46,XX females (in-utero androgen exposure) - normal male genitalia in 46,XY. Non-salt-wasting (25%): Partial enzyme deficiency. Only virilization

Lab: Elevated 17-OHP (diagnostic >1,000 ng/dL in newborns), Elevated androstenedione and testosterone, Low cortisol, Low aldosterone, Elevated ACTH, Elevated renin. Hypoglycemia, hyponatremia, hyperkalemia. Karyotype for genetic sex

Treatment: ACUTE ADRENAL CRISIS: Hydrocortisone 50-100mg/m² IV bolus then 100mg/m²/day IV divided q6h, NS 20mL/kg bolus, Correct hypoglycemia (D10W 2-4mL/kg), Correct hyperkalemia (Calcium gluconate for cardiac protection, Insulin + glucose, Kayexalate, NaHCO3). CHRONIC: Glucocorticoid replacement (Hydrocortisone 10-15mg/m²/day divided TID), Mineralocorticoid replacement (Fludrocortisone 0.1-0.2mg daily), Sodium supplementation 1-2g/day. Stress dose steroids during illness/surgery

Surgery: Feminizing genitoplasty for 46,XX females with ambiguous genitalia. Timing controversial (early 3-6 months vs delayed until patient can consent). Multidisciplinary team (endocrinology, urology, gynecology, psychology). Fertility possible with good metabolic control
